     The Numbers Business Report

The Mauritanian (2021)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $836,536Details
International Box Office $6,526,082
Worldwide Box Office $7,362,618
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Captured by the U.S. Government, Mohamedou Ould Slahi languishes in prison for years without charge or trial. Losing all hope, Slahi finds allies in defense attorney Nancy Hollander and her associate Teri Duncan. Together they face countless obstacles in a desperate pursuit for justice. Their controversial advocacy, along with evidence uncovered by formidable military prosecutor, Lt. Colonel Stuart Couch, eventually reveals a shocking and far reaching conspiracy.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$163,789 (19.6% of total gross)
Legs:5.11 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:11.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:248 opening theaters/287 max. theaters, 5.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $983,770

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 12th, 2021 (Limited) by STX Entertainment
International Releases: February 19th, 2021 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
February 26th, 2021 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
March 17th, 2021 (Wide) (South Korea)
March 19th, 2021 (Wide) (Spain)
May 13th, 2021 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
Video Release: March 2nd, 2021 by STX Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for violence including a sexual assault, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2657 (cert #52925), 11/25/2020)
Running Time: 129 minutes
Geographical setting: Guantanamo Bay, Cuba
Plot point: Prison, Wrongfully Convicted
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: SunnyMarch, Wonder Street, 30 West, Topic , BBC Films, STX Films, Great Point Media, Shadowplay Features
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

Captured by the U.S. Government, Mohamedou Ould Slahi languishes in prison for years without charge or trial. Losing all hope, Slahi finds allies in defense attorney Nancy Hollander and her associate Teri Duncan. Together they face countless obstacles in a desperate pursuit for justice. Their controversial advocacy, along with evidence uncovered by formidable military prosecutor, Lt. Colonel Stuart Couch, eventually reveals a shocking and far reaching conspiracy.
